name: OpenBrain – Personal Knowledge Operating System
summary: Designed and built a self-hosted personal knowledge operating system that captures observations, decisions, and source material from AI coding sessions, stores them in PostgreSQL with pgvector for semantic search, and exposes recall through a FastAPI service and an MCP gateway — enabling AI clients like Claude to remember and retrieve context across sessions. Architected with a layered service model (MCP → FastAPI → pgvector), brain_id isolation for future multi-user support, and a database-backed job queue for background ingestion and freshness review.
outcome: Running on a personal NAS; enables persistent AI memory and semantic recall across Claude Code, Claude Desktop, and custom agent workflows
notes: Personal side project demonstrating hands-on applied AI systems design — MCP integration, vector search, and self-hosted LLM tooling outside of client work.

name: MigrateIQ – AI-Assisted Migration Accelerator
summary: Architected an AI-powered data migration accelerator using BGE-M3 semantic embeddings and LLM-assisted field disambiguation with auditable decision trails stored in SQL Server. Reduces manual schema mapping effort by 80–85% while maintaining full traceability, confidence scoring, and analyst review workflows. Applied across multiple ERP-to-cloud migration engagements.

name: EDI Transformation & Exception Management Platform
summary: Designed and delivered a Boomi + SQL workflow for managing EDI transaction exceptions — including automated detection, structured review queues, approver workflows, correction actions, and reprocessing pipelines — for a multi-entity manufacturing client. Reduced manual exception resolution time by over 50%.

name: Documentation Acceleration Agents
summary: Developed a suite of Claude-based documentation agents that convert raw intake materials — meeting notes, scope documents, and data dictionaries — into structured project briefs, RACI matrices, and reusable knowledge artifacts. Cut new project kickoff documentation time from 3 days to under 4 hours.

name: DEQS – Data Extraction & Quality Suite
summary: Designed an internal data comparison and validation toolkit capable of processing CSV, JSON, and Excel schemas side-by-side to identify structural mismatches, field mapping gaps, and data-quality anomalies prior to migration. Improved pre-migration issue detection rate by ~60% across tested projects.

name: Accounts Payable Automation & ERP Integration
summary: Designed and deployed automated accounts-payable workflows integrated with client ERP systems to eliminate invoice processing backlogs, enforce approval hierarchies, and improve payment timeliness. Reduced manual AP touchpoints by ~40% and improved on-time payment rates within the first quarter post-deployment.

name: Hospital Pricing Transparency & Medical Billing Automation
summary: Supported a regional hospital network's compliance with HIPAA pricing transparency mandates by designing charge master reconciliation workflows, automated claims alignment processes, and service-data validation pipelines. Helped the client meet regulatory deadlines while reducing billing discrepancy rates.

name: FP&A Modernization – Snowflake + Power BI
summary: Led a finance and performance analytics modernization engagement for a $200M+ commercial services firm, migrating from legacy spreadsheet-based reporting to a Snowflake data warehouse with Power BI semantic layers. Delivered self-service executive dashboards, operational KPI tracking, and automated variance reporting.

name: State Insurance Department – Provider Spatial Analytics
summary: Designed a spatial analytics platform for a state insurance department covering provider specialty distribution, facility access gaps, and network adequacy across hundreds of locations statewide. Enabled regulators to identify underserved markets and produce compliance-grade network coverage maps.

name: Financial Services Governance & Controls Playbooks
summary: Supported governance transformation for multiple Tier 1 financial-services clients (assets ranging from $50B to $2.4T), developing controls libraries, remediation playbooks, data governance standards, and operating model documentation in response to OCC examination findings and federal consent orders.

name: AI-Assisted RFP Response System
summary: Developed a modular AI workflow system for accelerating RFP responses: requirement extraction agents parse incoming RFPs, capability-matching modules align them to approved firm content, and template-aware generation agents draft structured responses for human review. Reduced average proposal turnaround from 5 days to under 2 days.

name: 12-Agent Documentation Orchestration System
summary: Architected and implemented a 12-agent documentation orchestration system covering intake processing, validation, orchestration control, research synthesis, schema mapping, technical design generation, and quality review. Uses BGE M3 embeddings for semantic routing and an AI schema bridge for cross-agent context sharing.

name: MCP-Enabled Development Workflow Integration
summary: Integrated MCP (Model Context Protocol) server tooling into internal development workflows, connecting Claude-based AI assistance with file systems, project databases, and external APIs via VS Code. Established repeatable development patterns enabling team members to leverage AI assistance within governed, structured workflows.

name: Local LLM Evaluation & Workflow Experimentation
summary: Systematically evaluated local LLM deployments using Ollama and LM Studio to identify viable use cases for air-gapped client environments, cost-sensitive workloads, and latency-critical workflows. Produced an internal capability assessment comparing local models against hosted API options across accuracy, speed, and governance dimensions.

name: Enterprise Prompt Libraries & Agentic Workflow Patterns
summary: Developed structured prompt libraries, reusable agentic workflow patterns, and context-management templates for enterprise use cases including document automation, data review, governance reporting, and client communication drafting. Libraries are versioned, annotated, and organized by task type for team-wide reuse.

name: SOX Readiness & Data Architecture Remediation
summary: Assisted two mid-market companies in SOX readiness engagements, designing control frameworks, testing procedures, and data architecture improvements to address deficiencies identified during pre-IPO and annual audit cycles. Delivered remediation roadmaps adopted by internal audit and external auditors.

name: Private Aviation AI Agent Control Tower
summary: Led Boomi integration delivery and AI agent deployment for a private aviation group. Stood up five production AI agents on an Agent Control Tower with role-based access controls (RBAC), hallucination detection mechanisms, full audit trails, and observability dashboards. This represented a governed, enterprise-grade multi-agent deployment in a regulated operational environment.

name: Tiered PDF Extraction Pipeline – Financial Settlements Firm
summary: Designed and delivered an AI-powered tiered document ingestion pipeline for a financial settlements firm. The system applies layered extraction strategies — LLM prompt optimization as the primary layer, Azure Document Intelligence as the secondary layer, and Mistral OCR as a fallback — feeding structured data into Snowflake for analytics and reporting. Built to handle complex financial document formats with high accuracy and traceability.

name: AI Audit Agent Design & Audit Team Enablement ($1.9M ROI)
summary: Partnered with Audit & Assurance leaders to design and prototype AI Audit Agents applying agentic AI patterns to enhance audit planning, evidence gathering, and risk assessment workflows. Designed and delivered multi-phase enablement programs upskilling a 150-analyst audit and finance team on AI-enabled analytics tools. The program projected a ~$1.9M ROI impact through efficiency gains, reduced manual review time, and accelerated evidence preparation.
outcome: ~$1.9M projected ROI across 150-analyst team; reduced evidence prep and audit planning cycle times

name: Data Lake & Data Warehouse Controls Assessment
summary: Assessed design and operating effectiveness of Data Lake and Data Warehouse controls for major financial services clients. Scope included data dictionary governance, privileged access management, encryption controls, disaster recovery testing, and data lineage validation. Delivered findings and remediation roadmaps integrated into the client's internal audit program.

name: IRS Adoption Credit Calculation Automation
summary: While working as a Revenue Examiner at the Federal Revenue Authority, developed an automated process for calculating the Adoption Credit — a complex tax credit requiring multi-step verification across tax return data. The automation reduced calculation errors and manual effort significantly, earning Employee of the Month recognition. This was the first professional automation initiative, predating the formal technology and analytics career by several years.
notes: Early career project — relevant for demonstrating that the instinct to automate and build process efficiency has been consistent throughout the full career arc, not just the recent technology-focused years.

name: Enterprise Alteryx Server Governance Framework
summary: Built and operated the firm's enterprise Alteryx Server governance framework including role hierarchy design, Active Directory group management, service-account-only production publishing controls, and sandbox-to-production deployment process. Established the governance model that scaled the firm's Alteryx practice across multiple teams and client engagements.
